Several around library, including on the median behind the library by the book drop. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: June-June.

6 Juneberry/Serviceberry plants along the sidewalk at the entrance to the Moline Public Library.  There are also Juneberry plants in the medians around the parking lot.  The delicious berries are ripe in early June. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: June-J
Located along the Riverside Creek. Just a couple of canes that have vined up into a tree right off the sidewalk.

Wild black raspberries along the edge of the woods/tree line at Stephens Park in Moline, IL. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: June-August.

On Augustana campus, along the sidewalk and southwest of the old belltower Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: September-November.

Multiple Juneberry/Serviceberry plants surround Moline's Heritage Place at 1515 5th Avenue, Moline, Illinois.  The delicious berries are ripe around June 10th. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: June-June.
At entrance to the bridge at Sylvan Island Park. (Credit, Chris Rice) Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: June-unknown.
Apple. On city boulevard of the old Eagle/Rock Island Country Market store. August 2019, Green Apples with a pink blush. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: August-October.
At the Augustana College Local Agriculture Club food forest/orchard Falling Fruit season: August-September.
Apple Tree on an abandoned lot that is now owned by the city of Rock Island, Illinois. No one cares if you pick from it. Falling Fruit season: September-October.

A grafted Apple tree, one half is small berry- like crabapples, but the other half are small, sweet apples similar to a Wilson. Big enough to eat or peel. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: July-September.

Pear Tree on abandoned lot now owned by the City of Rock Island, Illinois. No one cares if you pick the fruit. Falling Fruit season: September-October.
